title: Aggregates nested inside a subquery join
prompt: Write `MyApp.Accounts.users_ranked_by_activity/0` that returns users with their total comments + total posts, ordered by the sum. Build a subquery that groups comments by user_id and selects `count(c.id) as comment_count`. Build another subquery for posts. Join both subqueries on the outer User query. Select `{u.id, u.name, comment_count, post_count, total}` with `total = coalesce(cc.comment_count, 0) + coalesce(pc.post_count, 0)`. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Two aggregate subqueries joined as sources — an advanced composition of subqueries + aggregates.
